review_text: Nice place to stop for lunch or coffee in Coffs harbour. Enjoyed the dutch cuisine. They also have all day breakfast and other lunch menu.
Love the clog barn! Great food at Oma's! We live at Coffs and always bring visitors here! Highly recommend!
Nice spot to visit and admire their mini village in the garden. Beautiful
An awesome show on how Clogs are made, very informative session.  The garden is also very good alot of history in there. Great place to visit.
Kids were really excited to visit here but it was super run down and no interactive displays worked. Work shed wide open for kids to dangerously enter

Nice little Dutch themed attraction. Was quite a surprise. Called in for coffee and got to wander through the back garden full of models of lots of famous buildings with descriptions, windmills and then a lovely gift shop.

We stopped off to soak up the Dutch charm for lunch only. Part of my Heritage is German/Holland. Brought back memories of my Nan when she would show us photos of Windmills and bring back little clog souvenirs.
I am now an Oma and my husband an Opa
Food was delicious and some traditional. Something for all.
I ordered the Dutch tasting plate. And hubby the Oma burger.
We then went and looked at the model town and met the Model maker and had a chat.
Absolutely beautiful model town, amazing detail and information.  Love love love.
I absolutely love this placs
Clean amenities, small, quiet site. WiFi is decent but there are some black spots. Plots could be marked clearly, but found easily with the map.

Cute experience seeing the mini Dutch village - very special as my family is from the Netherlands. They have all of the quintessential Dutch souvenirs and food. Loved the coffee, stroopwafels and the croquettes.
جای بسیار زیبا که میتونید صنایع دستی هلند رو پیدا کنید

We stopped for a break on a road trip for breakfast, which was excellent.
Particularly as it was pet friendly.
The miniature village is absolutely brilliant, and costs nothing.

The miniature models of different places in Holland gives a quick history and is spectacularly engaging for all ages. It has seen better days for sure, the trains were not working which was disappointingfor kids! The gift shop is pricey but has unique items that personally i havent found anywhere else..must visit if you are in Coffs Harbour!

Very unique Holland 🇳🇱 experience.
All the buildings are so realistic and the  yard is very neat and clean.  👌
Beautiful, beautiful,  beautiful.
Thank you for the time and effort you put into the project.
The waffles are so good! And Very friendly customer service
Great food
Food and services fantastic

Had a look through the souvenir shop with my Aunty. Beautiful items.
Afterwards for a donation we walked through the miniature village and garden which was wonderfully displayed and well maintained.
Had meal there Waiter's was sneezing all over our food

This place was amazing, centrally located not too far from Park Beach Plaza. The cafe has amazing pancakes, kids absolutely loved them. The gift shop had very reasonably priced souvenirs. The miniature garden has excellent model buildings and shown Dutch architectures and traditional culture - most importantly the experience is FREE! Sadly, we didn't stay here this time.
Great food, we drop in whenever we drive past!
Great little place to spend a day or two. Fun atmosphere with the little miniature town, pool and coffee shop. Would recommend for sure.

Best fried breakfast in town and the fluffiest pancakes anywhere.
Sit on the back deck and meat the local wildlife
It's a fun place to stop on a drive up or down to the coast. Kids will like the miniature village and the souvenirs but the accomodation and cafe aren't really worth talking about

Very Disappointing. The Restaurant was always our go-to  for Breakfast when we visited Coffs,,, Unfortunately things seem to have gone downhill ,, I ordered my Favourite Big Breakfast Pancake, and  it was a horrible glutinous mess,  The egg  looked  like a plastic trick egg and tasted like it to.  Absolutely inedible. My wife had eggs Benidict  and the egg was hard and over cooked (eggs Benidict should be a runny egg.) Would not recommend if this is what they dish up. Cook should have been ashamed to send it out.
Nice place  for the family
All my dutch products are there! ❤️
Nice and clean amenities,  staff are very friendly and helpful
Really cool place love my coffee mug i bought
Great little quirky place to stop off at when on the road.  The kids loved the miniature displayed and the Dutch food too!
Best pancakes and oma coffees
Beautiful shop and miniature displays. The clog making demonstration was brilliant!
Meals were delicious and amazing service

A delightful stop in Coff's Harbour! Great gift shop, miniature village and cafe!

🇳🇱 The owner came from Holland in the 50's and began work on the gorgeous miniature village out back the gift shop. He still comes in weekly to work on it, while his son runs the property.

👞 They make clogs on-site and great photo opps, too!

Had coffee and lunch here. Great Cafe outdoor seating near the pond. Kids were given bread to feed the fish.
Great selection of dutch food to order also Australian  as a meal and packaged foods to take home. The kids loved the little walk around  village with the train running through. The clogs were not being made when we were there but the workshop is visable so you can see. Lovely gift shop also.
Just wow. A must visit.

Loved the Dutch feeling of The Clogbarn! Great food and coffee, beautiful exhibit of ‘Holland’ in the back garden, and a little shop filled with nostalgia and beautiful things. The Cabin was clean and comfortable with all the amenities needed, and it was great to have a pool to cool off in!

Visited the museum and miniature villages, which are free and the souvenir shop, which is very cute containing a lot of the old clog manufacturing machinery.

This was my first visit in over 20 years of going to Coffs. Well worth a stop.

Did not stay or view the accommodation.
Food and service were great and quick. We will definitely go back in our next holiday.
Becareful with out if date stock.
Dutch miniatures are really good!

Cafe review: smoothies have ice cream in them and not shown on menu. When told my daughter couldn't drink it they didn't offer to replace it. Dutch mini pancakes were overcooked and dry. Bacon and eggs were nice.

Always come to the clog barn when in Coffs , staff are always great . Last day in Coffs we went for breakfast , the waffles are bang on . Credit to Zac for his customer service , recommend a pay rise . We will be definitely be back .
Village display out the back was interesting, the adjoining Cafe food was lovely
They let my dog into the shop!

Cute little clog shop with lovely products. The toys on display inside were very nostalgic, and the miniature buildings and sculptures were impressive, informative and captivating. The outside could use a little maintenance, as some interactive features weren’t working and the gardens need some attention. That said, I’d definitely recommend stopping by for a visit. It’s free!! And a great spot for people of all ages! 😊
Hermoso lugar para ver su ciudad en miniatura y llevar unos hermosos recuerdos
Cafe was lovely ! Food was great and coffee was wonderful!. The clog barn/garden was great to see
Was a pretty decent stay. Love the area with the miniature holland attraction and restaurant next door. Would stay again but hope they improve the cleaning- there were ants and spiderwebs everywhere.
很特別的景點，很像小人國的公園，免費可以捐贈。商品很漂亮，有荷蘭特色。旁邊有荷蘭特色咖啡廳。

We didn't stay at this motel. We visited the Clog Barn which is situated next to the main road.  Beautiful little barn with modelling of the buildings and landscaping resemble what you'll see in Holland.   Excellent job!!
Had a great stay here, stayed in a cabin. Very clean and newly refurbished. Will come back again.
Perfect to visit with family or loved ones. Would recommend to people crossing or visiting Coffs harbour .

Small area but cute
Free to visit

On your way back to Sydney from Coffs Harbour, this is a great place to take a break and admire beautifully crafted miniature city models.  It’s also dog-friendly so feel free to bring your furry friend along. Just be mindful of the limited parking.

There’s also a cafe next door where you can recharge before continuing your journey.
These people are so nice and helpful. Christmas/New year, nothing available in Coffs, they helped us out. Cabin was excellent.
Lovely food
For a great breakfast experience you gotta check this place out
Love this little tourist haven.  Very unique

Great place if you want to see some typical Dutch houses and taste some Dutch food (really, it’s said by a Dutchman 😉)! Good prices and they really sell a lot of Dutch stuff such as liquorous, appelstroop, pannenkoekenmix (pancake mix) and all other kinds of stuff. For the dutchies amongst us who are missing home: go take a trip down memory lane! The small houses are really cute and absolutely well made as well! Worth a visit! Didn’t stay in the park though so can’t say anything about that.
Only went to the shop. It was very interesting with the demo of the clog

We went to Oma's cafe for lunch and breakfast to enjoy some Dutch food and other items on the menu.
It was all a big hit!
We loved the miniature village, the Dutch liquorice that was for sale and other Dutch items.
Needs some maintenance

🕰️ A Charming Trip Down Memory Lane (Still Gorgeous, But Ready for a Glow-Up!)
We returned to this spot hoping to relive some childhood magic, and while, as expected, time has certainly marched on, this historic location still delivers a delightful dose of quiet charm!

The Old Girl Needs a Refresh

The site is still incredibly pretty, showcasing exceptional craftsmanship and historical detail that speaks volumes about its past life. If you love history, the detail alone is worth the trip! However, we could tell the old barn is visibly aged and a little worn around the edges—it's definitely ready for a refresher course! We genuinely hope future funding gives this unique historical gem the modern care and updates it deserves, but the character and beauty are absolutely still there to be enjoyed.

Nature's Quiet Cameo

The grounds are a wonderfully peaceful and quiet escape. While the main attraction itself doesn't take long to view (unless you commit to reading every fascinating historical plaque—and we recommend you do!), the real bonus was the wildlife. We were thrilled by nature's quiet cameo, spotting assorted lizards, birds, and dragonflies during our visit.

Just a friendly heads-up: Plan your timing wisely! It was already a scorching 32
∘
C when we arrived (and got far hotter), so bring a hat and water.

The Verdict: This visit was purely a nostalgia mission for us, and we left with warm, historical fuzzies! Truly, zero complaints—just appreciation for a peaceful moment in a historically beautiful setting.

There is also a caravan park and a dining option - neither were in our visit this time 😌

We are staying in Sawtell and spent the day doing some of the tourist things around Coffs Harbour.

Called in for a look, lots of clogs, Christmas items, German clocks and the usual tourist bits and bobs.

Able to grab meals and walk through the garden with awesome miniature buildings and train track.

There's a caravan park attached as well.
Well worth the visit. The village models were great and well tended. Food and coffee at the cafe was very good. Coffee, was very hot, so let it cool.
Wonderful  staff..great food and shopping

Stopped on my way from QLD to Sydney after a recommendation from Crossfire a colleague I work with who has stopped here with her family a couple of times, I stopped for breakfast (pancake 1/2 serve) which was amazing then went for a walk around the little gardens and shop
Overnight, 2 women camping in 2 cars. Charged one site for us both. Clean cruisy and nice people. Stopped northbound and again south.

We loved this place. Lots to see. Watch the clog making show.

Update 2025: visited again and loved it. Lots to see and buy. Would go again.

We visited the pit stop attraction of house figurines & cafe. It is very admirable that the owners put so much care and thought into this shop.

It is very calm and well maintained.

P.S. the lizards are real ! Not statues 🦎😬

Stayed at the caravan park here, amenities were really well kept.
Lots of fun dutch stuff to look at, very cute
Sadly the first room was dirty and had ants throughout. A second room was offered but still had ants in the bedding. Usually love visiting this place but won't be any more.
Lovely place for a visit with free entry to miniature Holland, Cafe was great, food was excellent
Lovely dutch food and miniature dutch village
Such a cute little shop! It has tiny miniature buildings inside — really charming and fun to look at.

We loved our visit to the Colg Barn.
For us it is a bit of nostalgia to see some of the gifts in the gift shop
Loved seeing the miniature village and enjoyed some of the Dutch fast food.
Of course we had to buy some liquorice.  Lol
Strongly suggesting of the free to visit with wonderful Miniature landscape。
It's good experience getting clog shop
Very friendly and helpful people.
Lovely place to visit and friendly staff.
Impressive little Holland at Down Under, we enjoyed our afternoon there.
Great place to stay all sizes of rooms/cabins available and the staff are fantastic shows it’s a family run place, really nice people, always willing to help or stop for a chat.

Great accomodation, good location close to shops, beaches and tourist attractions in Coffs Harbour. Clog barn was great nice Dutch themed place and amount of work to make look like it does is amazing. Definitely recommend to anyone to visit and stay very budget friendly too! My wife and myself enjoyed our stay here

We didn't stay in the hotel but did visit the clog barn.

It's a lovely place and a lot of work has gone into it. We really enjoyed it!
Definitely worth a visit.

It's a little tricky to get to without a car but 20 minutes walk from the city centre
Affordable with everything you need.

After decades of driving past, we finally decided to drop in and it was definitely worth it. Was great to walk around and look at the miniatures and the pancakes were absolutely amazing. Will definitely make sure to stop in again at the cafe in the near future 😃

Picture is of a half size serving of pancakes. Delicious 😍

Loved it. $45 for campervan, powered and choice of grass or slab. It's a very small friendly park in Coffs Harbour, basic, but clean. Wifi works, fast enough to stream. Parks tend to blur into each other after travelling a long time, however this one's so unique I'll remember it forever. The miniature village, free to visit, is well worth visiting even if you don't stay.
Ate breakfast (pancakes) at The Clog Barn, they were the best pancakes we have had in a long time. Service was excellent too. We will return.
Really cool place to visit and free
Nice dutch experience. Stocked up on dutch food, had a coffee in the cafe, bought some souvenirs and visited the miniatures exhibition of windmills etc.

Arrived at 2pm with 2 x families to eat the pancakes after 3 days of trying to get there.  Kids very excited.  Only to find they had closed the cafe early.  Building sign says 3pm close, Google's says 3pm close but they have already bailed.  Very unhappy kids
I absolutely love it , my parents where from Netherlands,  so l loved all of there items
I had crepes they were amazing

As a dutchie it was fabulous to revisit the clog barn after 30 years.  Display was still working and tidy with nice gardens. The cafe was terrific. The frikandellan was delicious 😋 Good experience all round. Tourist trinketary was vast but appropriate and reasonably priced.
The souvenirs are amazing and so many pairs of clogs. The mini Dutch Village out back is fantastic so much work put into it.
The Clog Barn is very quaint and the little village is lovely. The cafe next door with their pancakes was delicious, had to wait a little to get served but it was worth it
Leuke, kleine camping. Vooral grappig voor de Nederlanders. Warme douches en een kampeerkeuken.
It was a great place to have a breakfast sit alongside the creek. Enjoy eels and fish in the creek.
The most authentic display of Netherlands countryside and buildings that I have seen outside of the Netherlands. Detailed models are fantastic. The shop is straight out of Amsterdam.
Cleaner needs to do a better job she didn’t even mop the floors by the looks of it. Dog hair on blankets, exhaust fans filthy.
I was only visiting for lunch, and may I say yummo. Friendly service, yummy food great coffee.

Great set up out back with a mini town with windmills and trains . Gift shop was interesting with many different gift ideas. Went back 2cdays later for breakfast ,  we had amazing pancakes and creeps.  As my son said " way better than pancake  p...... "
Such a cool and unique place. The sites were good too. Really appreciate that its all dog friendly.
Great experience here and definately worth trying some authentic Dutch food in the cafe/resturant. Well worth the stop over at Coffs from the driving.
Great location. Didn't stay just visited the village

The cabin was fairly new and clean they were very easy to deal with had to move our stay a day forward nothing was a drama the owner was very welcoming and the kids loved the little village and clog shop had everything we needed for a quick stay
Love the Clog Barn. Clean rooms. Great prices. AWESOME food!

A great little spot in Coffs Harbour! The Clog Barn has a really welcoming atmosphere, delicious coffee, and a fun miniature Dutch village to explore. Loved watching the clog-making demonstration—such a unique experience. Definitely worth a visit!
Friendly staff and a clean park - did stay long as it was a quick layover but the Dutch ‘vibe’ was great.
Food and ambience very average. Menu needs to include some traditional Dutch vegetarian dishes.

Was an unexpected delight. Free admission to a full-on miniature Dutch village out the back (at least 60 miniature buildings) which is immaculately maintained, with working windmills and canals galore. Great, quirky gift shop with clogs, delft ware, colourful wooden tulips and cuckoo clocks! Worth a visit.
This is a great place to visit to see the miniature village. And it is free.  We had a lovely lunch there also.  It's definitely worth a stop.
We just went into the clog barn to check it out. There's a free miniature village created for visitors. Nothing particular to buy unless you want clogs or cuckoo clocks.

We had lovely pancakes and the young lady that served us was fabulous, pity about the rude older lady who looked like she did not want to be there and snarled we close at 3 and stomped off. The young lady was exceptional and polite and gave great service with a big smile . Highly recommend the berry pancakes they were awesome
An absolutely amazing experience. My boys loved it! The pancakes were to die for. Will be back!
Kids had so much fun!

Thankyou for having Alf,myself and our little dog Leo, Manager was so nice, helped locating our camper van, great personality.All staff were so nice, very inviting, especially loved hearing about our it all began 45 yrs ago.gift shop beautiful, as Always..first time in the Cafe for morning tea, was just yum..Thankyou again, love kim, Alf and Leo. If you don't remember me ..I had a word for the day
easy parking see the small buildings/models and the cuckoo clocks and clogs

Went to the tourist attraction which was a great experience. In the shop, there is a live demo of how a clog is made, which was very informative and interesting.
To view the miniature Dutch houses/ palaces / buildings etc and windmills, one has to go through the shop.
There is no entry fee to view the buildings. It is very well kept and lovely to see.
Always enjoy coming here when in town.
Just all you need. The staff and people who live here are very friendly
Nice place to Visit
Nice place,  free visit
kids loved it! The food is amazing as well, breakfast is good!
Great croquettes and eggs bennedict

Went for a visit, I believe the recent one star reviews are very wrong from what I experienced.
The pancakes were the size of the plate. We had excellent service and it was busy.

We went next door and met John, he will turn the train on for you. There is no point turning on the train when no one is out looking at the miniature houses as this just adds wear and tear.

Next we saw John make a clog in 5 minutes. The traditional way takes hours. We even got the demo clog.

The slippers are comfortable.

The Clog Barn is a very cute, free attraction in Coffs and well worth popping in to see. It’s very unique, as is the attached gift shop selling all things Dutch! You can even purchase your own set of clogs & there are many other beautiful gifts and ornaments for sale. There is a quaint little cafe next door also. Don’t forget the photo opp in the big clogs outside!
We didn't stay.  We visited the shop and Cafe.  The Clog making demonstration was enjoyable.
LOVED miniature houses and villages in dutch locations, all hand crafted to scale.
Can recommend going to the clog barn . Quite an interesting place seeing all the different Dutch attractions.
Cute little shop a miniature villages worth the stop.
Best value park in North NSW, excellent service,  beautiful clean pool. Lovely clean amenities, gardens and grounds.  Local residents are very friendly.  We will be back again.

Our family  like  to swim even in winter, pool locked, pool filthy, are the two people that stand on the corner smoking and looking homeless , the night managers???
Doesnt give the park a good look,
My family use to stay there and my husband and I visited them on there stay , we ate at the cafe which was lovely
But my family won't be returning unless this park is cleaned up, a nice polo shirt as a uniform would be nice
Very unhappy  🙁 ☹️ 😔

Nice little Dutch experience, with a store selling clogs, cuckoo clocks, and a miniature garden display at the back of buildings and a Thomas the tank engine train to complete the look. Try the coffee house next door too - delicious Dutch food, from their coffee through to their unique breakfast tasting platter
Nice place, cabin had been renovated and was nice and modern. Handy to everything.
Great for kids

Amazing food at Big Oma’s Cafe and we just loved the little village and great Dutch items.
Highly recommend stopping in for an amazing big breakfast

Stayed in caravan park, sites were a bit wet due to ongoing rain (not their fault) but were generous in size and fairly level. Amenities were very clean and well maintained. The Clog Barn itself had clogs,clogs & more clogs then there were the cuckoo clocks of varying degrees of styles plus clog making demonstrations. The Holland Downunder village was well designed and displayed realistic dwellings from towns in the regions of Holland all hand cut and hand painted from perspex. No entry for village walk just donation box at exit to assist with upkeep and maintenance. The cafe next door sells Dutch treats and foods both hot and cold plus good variety of hot & cold drinks, DON'T leave before having one of their milkshakes WOW, WOW, WOW that's all I will say.
Excellent time out and enjoying the little garden and buildings. Even find the local lizards all at home.
The croquettes are amazing - definitely worth the stop!
Thank you from the happy undertaker.
We only dropped by for a short visit while passing through, but it turned out to be the highlight of our family trip. We had a great time there with our little kids!
Worth a quick stopover if you're in the area

The Clog Barn in Coffs Harbour is a charming and unique experience! The free miniature Dutch village is captivating with its intricate details. Don't miss the fascinating free clog-making demonstrations. Big Oma's Coffee House offers delicious Dutch treats and meals. The gift shop has unique souvenirs. Friendly staff and a relaxed atmosphere make it a delightful visit for all ages. Highly recommended for a touch of Holland in Coffs Harbour!
If Google allows 10-star, I give definitely give this store 10-star. It’s Netherland in Australia.
Booked for 3 adults and 4 children. Glad the 3rd adult didn't come because it was a really tight fit. We won't book here again. Space was extremely minimal.
Nice miniatures and a shop with souvenirs
A fun little experience
Must see attraction
Food was okay, really nice waiter named Samir made the experience 100 time better. Gave the whole family a laugh and was super nice. Give this Samir guy a raise or something love that dude

The clog barn is one of the best places I have visited. It has really good customer service, but one person stuck out the most, a guy named Samir. He was really nice and kind and was pretty quick at their job. The food was fantastic.
I didn't stay there I takes the kids there for breakfast they love it I love and you can always find something to eat for everyone
Interesting little attraction. Great value entry price. Free. Look around the tiny village and through the gift shop. There is a cafe with Dutch products for sale.
Village very well displayed plus demonstration of Clog making.
Dog friendly store and mini museum
Very nice place to visit. I had waffles they were delicious. The little village was interesting. I would recommend.
The demonstration was amazing,  friendly place
Pancakes 🥞  are always on point and delicious 😋
Good food and good service
Stopped croquettes and tea. Lovely
The red-haired lady that served my coffee was a pleasure to interact with and made quite the delicious brew.
Wonderfully kind staff, older but clean and well kept facilities. Not crowded and a good location. Highly recommend!
Great place to visit with so many cute hill and themed gifts and accessories. Tiny gardens are a work of art and free to look at.

We came for the food (mini Dutch pancakes) and enjoyed the delightful atmosphere in the cafe. We sat outdoors where the lovely sounds of nature helped make our morning even more delightful. It was a nice, quiet space to enjoy yourself before you set off to your next destination.

We didn't stay here but just went in to see the miniature village and the restaurant. It was fun to see our hometown Arnhem and het duivelshuis. Arnhem is spelled wrong on the Elden sign (the small village surrounded by Arnhem, not Arnham).

There's a small shop with Dutch souvenirs and you can buy Dutch food items in the restaurant. Of course highly overpriced, compared to what they cost at the Jumbo supermarket in the Netherlands. But it's fun to buy something Dutch in Australia if you're travelling around for a very long time.

The restaurant offers kroket, smaller than the original ones in the Netherlands. But we were craving for it. It was great fun having a 'broodje kroket'!
We didn't stay, but really enjoyed the miniature village, gift store and morning tea in the Cafe on site next door.
Really friendly owner, very good water pressure in the showers. Clean amenities.
Was awseome no cost on entry we had lunch in Cafe get service and lovely food 😋
Have stayed there many times, clean and convenient

The people here are just wonderful. The cafe, model village and the clog demonstration are all great and worth the visit.

The pricing of the clog slippers was a bit on the nose but we didn't mind paying it given the entry is free

If your seeking something different and fun the clog barn stay is for you, featuring a great little souvenir shop and a cute tiny walk through world.
The cabin we stayed in was very clean and tidy just a little on the small side.
But the best part is it’s conveniently located 5mins away from the best dumpling bar.

We just dropped in on our way through to this beautiful little place. A Dutch bonanza of a gift shop with an amazing miniature village out the back, that’s Free to walk through! Thoroughly recommend stopping in if you’re travelling through or to Coffs Harbour.
Lovely place to get close to Dutch village and learn about clogs. Definitely recommended if you have kids
Amazing effort by staff and owner
Reception was very friendly, however on the day we had lunch, the waitress was sullen and disinterested

The place is cute with the walk through of the mini buildings, and clog barn is nice with loads of history and souvenirs.
The cafe is nice so was the food. However, the staff were quite rude whilst I understand it can get busy, we are human and still deserve to be spoken to kindly.

It is such a great place to stay if you are travelling through.  There's a fairy garden to take the kids, pool on site, and it's pet friendly!!

Early shop and breakfast opening mean you can get a souvenir or a few, have some frikendel or dutch pancakes, and enjoy Coffs or continue on your journey!

Above all, it is very affordable,  friendly staff and local owners.  I highly recommend staying here!!

Cabins riddled with ants 🐜. Beds not made when checked in . Not enough towels to accommodate each guest we had and when asked why the beds were made just gave us sheets and told to make them up our selves worse stay cation in coffs harbour. By far

Enjoyed our stay here. Really good location, just down from the shopping centre and tourist attractions. The site was a good size and the park staff were so lovely and friendly. The clog making demonstration was really cool to watch, and we enjoyed walking through the mini village out the back. Would stay here again in the future ☺️

It was a last minute booking, these people were so lovely!||Cabins are clean, air con is cold and we had a great time.||We stayed here after our booking was cancelled by the windmill hotel in Coffs Harbour, the windmill hotel cancelled our booking, gave our room away and tried to charge us.||They did not care at all.
Good fun, friendly staff

Stayed at a powered campsite. We had a concrete slap under the awning. Tap, power, grey waste and dump point on our site. Toilets and showers were very clean and no one was ever in there.
We had breakfast at Oma’s restaurant every morning and it was great value for money and the food was delicious.  We tried to try something different every morning but the Poffertjes were too hard to resist.
There’s also Dutch supermarket food you can buy along with a gift shop.

We did not stay there. What we did do was visiting the shop, the miniature Dutch building and ate at the cafe. The food at the cafe was pretty decent although I was not a big fan of those meatballs and the nuggets were vile bordering poisonous

My husband and I went to the clog barn and Cafe. We watched the guy make some clogs which he was very informative with.  Lovely place and some nice wooden items to buy,  I bought some beautiful tulips.  The staff in the barn were friendly and helpful.  The outside model village was a little gem and free to walk round.  The Cafe next door was nice but the staff were rude. Would recommend a visit to the barn
Great place to visit. Ice cream great
Loved the miniature village
Absolutely magical looking around the miniature village.
stopped by for breakfast. excellent food. great price. staff were great. minature village is worth a look.

We didn’t stay at the accommodation but we visited the Clog Barn shop. It’s a lovely place to visit.

There are cute and interesting Dutch products, especially the coocoo clocks.

Don’t forget to check out the miniature garden at the back of the shop. They minimize historical buildings and important buildings with some descriptions. I love the details that they put in the work.

There’s also a cafe next door with very homey ambiance. You can either sit indoor or outdoor. The food looks yummy. We bought Germany and the Netherlands snacks from here. ❤️❤️
Good coffee and food was good
Interesting miniature village. Great gifts and the clocks are amazing!

The cabins are small and basic but clean and comfortable enough. But the gift shop, cafe and miniature village really make it memorable. We loved looking through the shop and watching the clog making demonstration. The staff were all very friendly. It is also very close to attractions and shops so a good place to stop in Coffs Harbour.

Nice place!
Nice souvenirs
An interesting selection of miniature buildings. Cafe serves a great selection of Dutch food.

We booked 3 nights and paid upfront. Firstly, our original booking was given away to people arriving before us, who also asked for our key to the ammenities as they didn’t get one. Then, on our last day after coming back from the beach we noticed others were set up on our site. Turns out they had given our site to someone else that was passing through town! When we tried to sort things out the lady advised that we should leave our personal belongings on site so they can see someone is booked. Apparently the fact that we had booked and paid for our site was irrelevant to them.
Complete chaos and disorganised!!
On the positive, the amenities are clean and the sites are well maintained.

It was a last minute stay, the receptionist was so lovely and helpful, the cabin we stayed in was very clean and I would recommend staying here!

We will be back.
Always come here for breakfast when I’m in the area. A definite must visit.
Management & the permanent tenants were lovely & kind. Cabin & pool was spotless. All round great value. (Electric stove in cabin 7 needs fixing). Thank you for having us!
beautiful set up and well looked after

Great place to stop over. The rooms are basic, modern and clean with everything you need.
Staff are welcoming and friendly..The food at Oma's is top notch 🙌
The gift shop and clog demonstrations are a nice way to end the day or start the day even.
Will visit again for sure ❤️

Just because it’s Holiday time, their prices just went rocket High!
I found $130 for a standard Cabin, i tried to book but it says the selected dates not available, and the calendar there is a call sign.
I called and said wanted to book the standard cabin, blahhh.. Blahhhh
He said there is an available $245 and I said It says in the website $130?
He said don’t trust the website, there is No $130, and because it’s Holiday.

Okay! Bye!
Won’t book on you ever again!
Loved this place
Lemon sorbet nice
I's not until you wander out the back of the shop that you see the magic.  Our highlight of Coffs Harbour.

Lovely food Great place to to visit Amazing display of some of the Dutch History
Clogs were amazing and made on site Lots of food and gifts to buy
Quaint lovely spot to appreciate a different culture.

To be honest the place looks very pretty and welcoming from the front but once you pass that little house omg the room I stayed it smelt horrible and I felt trapped it was stinking hot and the air con would not work people come to help us turn the tv on and I was laying on my bed when the worker sat down and nearly squashed my feet we left politely before the night and told the workers we had to leave urgently would not recommend if you hate smelly hotels didn’t even attempt to eat at the cafe I was out of Coffs Harbour before you could say the word Coffs

Came to the clog barn excited for the experience since it was my partners favourite childhood memory from Coffs and wanted some clogs badly. The cafe was pretty good, decent service and good value meals - nice to look at everything around the store. We went around the handmade models which were amazing and very well done. The disappointing part is being in the gift shop and being ignored by all 5 people that appeared to be staff members and unfortunately leaving without clogs. I stood behind the staff member at the counter, got a glance but that was all. In the time we took our shoes off, found our size (took a bit), waited around and eventually put our shoes back on, they still didn’t acknowledge us so we decided to go.

The caravan park has all you need, including local, helpful characters. As we were bringing our own caravan, I can't speak to their lodgings, but their facilities are immaculate. Oh, and the dryer doesn't need more than $2 to dry the average load. Lovely people, and the clog museum is delightful.

The Clog Barn was so beautiful! (I didn’t stay here - i just see the attractions and the cafe) , The tiny miniature houses remind me like the olden days of the people , The cafe was so good , Love Clog Barn
Great Dutch food
Only stopped here for brekkie and it was magic.
This was very informative and entertaining. Great friendly folk. A must see if you're in Coffs.

Lovely little visit to this quirky little place.
Great little cafe & gift shop.
Interesting miniature Dutch village out the back.
Only negatives bit noisy at night
The cabin was mostly clean,but the shower wasn't clean & the shower curtain had dirt & mould on the bottom of it.
The locals living there make the place looks a bit messy.

came by to see their miniature buildings, it was really nice for a stretch & stroll after a long drive. Clogs were beautiful and regretted not purchasing a mini one.
Everybody in the store is super friendly and helpful. Can be a good first stop if you are visiting coffa
Stunning place 🤩.
Felt a bit uncomfortable as some customers gave a rasict look. That is just my opinion.
Camper van overnight stay. Friendliest welcome yet. Great site. Quirky and all amenities close as small site. John and all staff super friendly and helpful. Recommend highly thank you.
Beautiful little gem hidden in the middle of Coffs Harbour. Definitely recommend a visit!
家的感觉很舒适很安心的一晚
Lovely little tourist attraction. Free entry. Donation welcomed. Unique clog barn shop.
Great little German Barn with a Dutch Village to walk around they even run the Caravan park next to it, loved the Cuckoo Clocks!!!.
Unfortunately it's not worth the visit as it has nothing to offer in both the souvenir shop and Cafe

Awesome clog making demonstration by the founder's son. He explained everything about the process of making wood clogs. We went through the lovely little Holland miniature village behind the shop. With such a convenient location and so many interesting things, definitely put it on your list if you are in Coffs Harbour. The Cafe (Big Omas's house) also served delicious and affordable Australian and Holland food.

It's a great tourist attraction for the whole family. Well kept gardens and friendly staff. Cool, little gift shop, too.

Highly recommended for a fun family day out whole in coffs harbour.

Friendly wildlife throughout the attention.
Good food and coffee.good service.
Good breakfast before heading back to Sydney.

Bed was so comfortable! Cabin had been renovated so was clean and comfy. My dog loved it. Good facilities and parking. Staff were great both for the park and the Cafe. Quirky full time residents but all lovely.

Awesome / clean toilet facilities for pitstop & lovely Eggs Bennedict.
Very lovely laid back & friendly staff.
Great place to visit while on holidays.

Booked last minute, they were so accommodating and nothing was an issue at all, the rooms were wonderful, clean and comfy.

Close to everything we needed. We definitely will be back.

Rooms are clean and there is every facility available like a kitchen, bathroom, T.V, refrigerator etc.
Enough space for parking. Truck can be parked over there.
Enjoy the beautiful park view.
Nice spot have a look at the tiny village  can get a meal there as well

If you are ever in Coffs Harbour NSW?
Make sure you visit the Clog Barn and enjoy a very unique experience.
This was a unique experience,  a fun family destination that you won't find anywhere else.

Take the time to stroll through the gift shop and miniature village, it's all very well made with little snippets of what the buildings are all about.
Plenty of water dragons sunning themselves for the little kids to spot
The Cafe is a delight, definitely put it on your list to eat out at. Not overly big (10 inside tables), which is what makes it appealing. The service is great, friendly and timely. Meal sizes are great
Went back because we couldn't stop thinking about it.

Right on the main road this cute place has plenty to offer. Enjoyed seeing the miniature Dutch village models at the backside of the clog barn. Saw the 15 mins clog making demo at 10 am by son of the clog barn founder. Browsed the clog shop for some gifts. Had breakfast at clog barn cafe and got to know about the history of the clog barn from the grand daughter who took our table order. Admirable team work by all family members for 22 yrs in this family owned business.

Had breakfast in the Cafe, local told me it was the place to get a good feed. Food was good, big breakfast slightly more expensive than other places but the food was good. Some small touches like proper leaf tea was a nice touch. Went into the clog workshop, massive amount of stuff on offer. The little Netherlands display was a wonderful touch and you can see the pride they take with it.
Great place for a bite to eat, coffee and check out the mini village and clog display which is free. I haven't checked out the rooms so I couldn't rate them.
Beautiful miniature Village. Great food in the Cafe , nice souvenir shop and very friendly staff
Great place with lots of dutch attractions and merchandise. The croquets are almost as good as in Holland.
We only visited the miniature village which was free of charge.
Very pleasant staff, nice meals and family orientated. Good location with plenty of level parking. We are local so didn't use the accommodation.
Always well worth a visit, the shop, Cafe & the model village particularly.  Great staff support.   A wonderful investment in time to experience this attraction.
Not bad little cafe
Was such a nice place to visit, the miniature village was fantastic
Beautiful Dutch apple cake for afternoon tea followed by a very interesting walk through the Miniature Village. We learnt a lot about Dutch history as well as seeing some very tame water dragons.

The light lunch meals were nice and well priced. There's lots to look at and the kids enjoyed the minature village and wandering around the shop. So much to see is lots of fun and helps to enjoy the day. Remember clog making demo is only on at 10am and 2pm.
A colourful and interesting journey through the miniature village and gift shop. Got coffee in the Cafe where we were well looked after in comfort with a bush outlook.
Simple clean stopover accomodation located centrally with onsite activities.
I like this place. So beautiful and reminds my childhood memories 😍 there are many handicrafts with reasonable price. Entree for miniature village is free.
Seru
work of art in the display village
Didn't stay here but went to their little cafe/restaurant. Great coffee and food. So many authentic Dutch treats. Well worth stopping here if you are driving through or staying in Coffs region.

Really well looked after the Freindly Service was a breath of fresh air Really
Loads of Dutch Favorite s too.
Fantastic experience seeing the houses, castles and windmills all made by hand and the food at the cafe was amazing!!
Didn’t stay there so not sure about rooms but the old world souvenirs are cool
good for the price 👍 very clean ,friendly place
Visited a souvenir shop, which has lots of items for sale.

Un loc foarte interesant, unde am descoperit un orășel olandez, în miniatură. Un loc frumos, cochet, unde totul era tipic unui oraș European. Exista un mic magazin de unde puteai achiziționa diferite produse specifice acestui loc. Mi-a plăcut tare mult vizita în acest loc și am fost foarte impresionată de detaliile care au făcut ca totul să arată atât de bine.
Absolutely beautiful place ❤️ yummy food and the service was excellent.

We had a powered grassed caravan site, first impressions are an out dated 1980's looking  trailer  park.  But although very  old  the ammenities are spotless.  It was a very good place to stay with great proximity to a shopping centre, cinema and almost every fast food place you can think of. I would stop there again.  The staff could not do enough for us.  A+ thank you.
The gardens were so beautiful and enjoyable to walk around. Nice gift shop too and very friendly staff. Great place to buy presents!
Kids enjoyed looking at the models.. the shop was busy but it was great learning about Dutch History and then seeing and tasting Dutch snacks etc

We just stopped for lunch on the way to Port Macquarie. The food was generous, delicious and reasonably priced, and the ambience in the cafe was very homely. Will definitely eat here again next time I'm in Coffs Harbour

We booked a standard cabin to stop and rest overnight on our long drive home.We arrived and the staff were friendly and helpful but when we arrived in our room it was extremely disappointing. The beds were thin vinyl and the sheets didn’t seem to be very clean. There was mould around the air vents above the main bed and water damage on the walls. The couch was very unclean to the point I had to tell my children not to touch it. The main issues however was that it seemed as though glen 20 or someone other type of cleaning spray had been sprayed excessively throughout the cabin which caused respiratory issues for us and that we could hear one of the permanent residents behind us yelling and swearing at someone. In the end we decided to have a quick rest and get back in the car and continue our drive home that night instead of staying.

The overall attraction was good but the meal we had was left three quarters uneaten which speaks for itself, my creamy prawn crepe was anything but. you couldn’t cut the prawns with a knife let alone your teeth, micro job red hot.
My wife had chicken asparagus and it was also stringy and the chicken was red hot micro heated again, stick with the burgers don’t expect French style crepes.
We just stopped in at the barn tourist attraction and mini Dutch village. The kids are part Dutch and they loved it and the shop. Highly recommend if you are coming to Coffs harbour
Worth paying a visit to this place. I have never been to Holland, but the intricately made scale models built there make you feel you have seen it in person.

Got gifted a pair of comfortable, lovely clogs last year when my family stopped by.

This year we were only staying for a short time, but couldn't miss the opportunity to stop at the Clog Barn!

Very friendly staff, beautiful well maintained display/diorama out the back and more clogs than you could handle!

Had a scoop of ice cream here too, which was also lovely!

Definitely recommend stopping by if you're in the area!
So great that they allow dogs out on the back deck and also in the miniature display village. A cute little cafe to visit.

Either as a child, or adult... this place never dies.
From clog making, the (free to enter) miniature Village, lovely gift shop, grandma's restaurant or getting that memorable 📸 photo standing in the clogs out front lol..
The Clog Barn is a monument, alongside the Big Windmill to the rich Dutch heritage within the Mid nth coast.
Definitely worth a visit ☺ 👣🍰☕
Nothing's perfect so I rate this attraction 8.5 out of 10.

10/10 experience, love the clogs and bought so many.

Naomi in the Cafe was amazing and the food was delicious, great apple juice selection by Naomi.
Great selection of Dutch meals, sweets, licorice as well as tourist gifts.

Very poor experience both times I visited, 2 of the staff were friendly (the cook and the young waitress) and the other definitely did not want to be there. had a birthday breakfast with my family and didn’t see the lady smile once when she took our drink order all she said was ‘yep’ the whole time, we waited 15 min for half the drinks to come out cold when we were the first ones to order as soon as they opened. Our pancakes were near raw in the middle and soggy and our extras for our pancakes came out 5 min later. After paying I had asked for a receipt as I needed to see the prices and the lady froze for a sec and told me ‘ah, possibly not, my printers not working right now’ I then asked whether they do an email or text receipt and she was unsure then told me to right down my email and she’ll send it to me when it’s back up and running, definitely wouldn’t go back
Quaint little clog store... a welcome rest look and wander
Very cute

Miniature village models are such a joy to see.  Interesting historical facts were also mentioned alongside the models. The gift shop is beautiful and colourful. We got some souvenirs as well. A must do!
Quaint little stop at the shop and you can buy clogs

Wow!.
What a lot of hard work has done into this.
Amazing

Fun place with and amazing miniature town good food and  service.

Only issue on this visit was witnessing how a person, we were advised was the owner, spoke to the staff member helping us, which was very rude and unprofessional in my view.

Unbelievable...what a place!!
Little village is A-MA-ZING!!
BRILLIANT.

Enjoyable lunch.
Something different, wth simple but tasty Dutch food.
The little store had a wide variety of Dutch food items. Worth a look.
Soo much to see  A beautiful place
Don’t mind getting there a bit before closing, they shut down the kitchen at least 20 minutes before they close.
Food, Beer and atmosphere were all great! Amazing staff too!

Our family stopped here for a bite to eat and it did not disappoint. Great little gift shop and the coffee shop had delicious pancake options which we loved. The little village was amazing and so well taken care of, it was a delight! We look forward to stopping there again next time we are in Coffs Harbour.
Cute little place. Coffee is the best so nice we came to have it twice.
Great place to visit! Lots to see. Thank you

Stayed 2 nights, staff were pleasant,  cabin was neat and tidy well priced, very small bed though. Only thing that put me off was that our cabin was surrounded by permanent residents that were very untidy with mess all around their residence. Unfortunately for this reason wouldn't stay again.
These guys really know how to connect with the kids.
Must stop for anyone with kids. Absolute gem of a place, the dutch village at the back is simply marvellous

Our doggos were welcome outside at the Cafe that served Dutch fare!  We all enjoyed a quiet walk through the Miniature Dutch village, and the souvenir shop has cute and useful items.  I'm going to get myself some cool wooden clogs from here on our next trip, as they provide a clog making service inclusive of checking the fit.
Beautiful stop where you can get some lovely breakfast... Gift items.. Clogs... Cuckoo clocks... And they have a beautiful garden at the back...
Great place to relax and enjoy a meal and see the garden displays

We visited the Clog Barn in April and saw the tiny Dutch Village and a Clog Making demonstration. It was very interesting. Due to recent rain, the miniature railway wasn't running.
I can highly recommend the food at Big Oma's Coffeehouse! We went back 2 days in a row. Lovely location.

Un orășel in miniatura care reprezintă casele din Olanda și de unde exista un magazin tipic olandez pentru ați cumpăra diverse accesorii si magneti de firigider. Multă lume se înghesuie sa vina sa facă poze. Sunt incantat de de am găsit aici. Locația am găsit-o pe google iar din review-urile văzute am luat decizia buna să venim aici. Dacă vreți să faceți o incursiune în Europa atunci acesta este locul potrivit.

Stopped here for a lunch driving break in April 2024 and really enjoyed. Great little gift shop, feels like you are in Europe with all the cool tourist items including of course clogs. Each day clogs are made live too. The Clog Barn also has a cafe and miniature village, both fantastic too!
Great place to stop on the drive down the coast if you have kids in the car.

We only got to the shop
FABULOUS , huge range of goods & pricesof, obiviously dutch products.  There is a wonderful surprise dutch village model garden out the back , not to massive to get around, really enchanting with a small model train going around in 1 area.
Staff terrific, pity we hadn't time for the cafe.

Well worth a look see.  👍

We popped in as it’s was on our way driving.
Such a beautiful little garden with lots of different little houses and designs that was amazing to see and explore. Also got chance to see how they made shoe out of wood.
Labour of love from the previous generation. All handmade models of places in Holland, there is a cafe and shop onsite as well as a caravan park.

Just visited the model village and shop. Village worth a look especially as there is no charge (donations appreciated). Shop has some good items including Christmas decorations. Cafe food was average.
Was a very interesting place, they also do Clog making demonstrations

The clog barn was very cool. Packed full of all your clog needs.
The Cafe next door was full of goodies not usually found in Australia.
There is also a "tiny town" tucked away out the back of the clog barn. A must see!
My partner and I were in the shop for a fair while ,the 2 ladies behind the counter didn't even take any notice of us no hello or good bye,other than that the place was so còol

We've always meant to stop off and see this attraction and finally got around to it today.  Fascinating Dutch themed store featuring clogs and associated items you'd expect to find.  We loved the little model town you can wander around at the rear of the store. Next door is a cafe, not the greatest coffee I've ever had, but the carrot cake is definitely worth trying, and the service is really lovely.

Congratulations  to the team at the Glogbarn for their exceptional  service
The pancakes are so nice and fluffy.
The best on the coffs coast
Super great pancakes.

Great service
Delicious food.

The lounge was smelly and so stained 🤢 ,had a dirty floor and a bad smell inside the cabin. Needs a major update .I had a superior cabin ( cheapest) and will not be returning any time soon. Bed was ok as was the bathroom.
Get your Dutch on! Big variety of delft blue ornaments for sale. Food was yummy and the Miniature village was well done.
Very interesting venue, miniature village is amazing

The clog barn is a great place to visit. You can see clogs being made, there are plenty of clogs to buy including clog slippers. Lots of Dutch souvenirs and out back is a miniature Dutch town to walk around. Great place.
Amazing food
Quirky place filled with fun and lots of imagination

Being Dutch myself I can honestly say I was impressed by the amount of accurate dutch things they have collected to sell. The frikandellen speciaal I would recommend very very dutch and it taste the same as in the Netherlands!
Nice place to visit, so much souvenirs to buy, a short history of The Netherlands
Love every bit of it, well worth going
A cute little driving break travelling from the Gold Coast to Sydney. Quirky souvenir shop, awesome model train set up outside, Cafe and clean toilets.

Stayed here and will definitely stay again. The miniture village is a lovely addition. We didn't eat here as we were able to cook meals in our cabin which was a huge bonus for us. Shops are within an easy walking distance. Great location.
Didn't stay there, but visited the miniature village. Was wonderful. The kids enjoyed looking at the mini buildings and windmills!

Beautiful place with miniature model village. The shop has German cuckoo clocks, Holland souvenirs and wooden clogs. 2nd visit here on way to Brisbane. Had picked up a cuckoo clock in my first visit 2 years ago and the store owner was very helpful with explaining how to set it up.
Such a beautiful set-up and friendly staff. Cafe is great lovely food and awesome coffee.
Loved this place my parents owned a cabin there and my kids just loved it. The pool.the location is great for families.  Great memories for me.

It's one of the best places for the minature #Holland Downunder We have never seen such type of innovative minature design.also, there is a beautiful clog barn shop where we can see the buy the things This place is clean and well maintained, kids, friends and family, and enjoy.
Awesome food great value and portions tasted awesome 😎
There wasn't really much to see, but looking at the little house models we're great and the gift shop was pretty cool. It's a nice place to stop by at and have a look.

Awesome place to stop.on your way through Coffs Harbour. The miniature Dutch village is pretty cool with some delicious food options in the Cafe next-door. Make sure you time you visit around 10:00 or 2:00 so you can watch the clog making demonstration as it is surprisingly educational and a lot of fun. Definitely don't drive past this place, it's right on the main road through Coffs 😄
Had breakfast here with my family,  cute little place, good pancakes and service.
